mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly boosting equity valuations over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above 4.5%, it signals deteriorating labor market conditions that compress consumer spending and corporate revenue expectations, leading to negative equity returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day forward returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment threshold breach.

mechanism_narrative: The joint contraction of real GDP growth and rising consumer credit balances indicates a synchronized demand shock that overrides monetary policy support, depressing equity valuations over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The interaction term (GDPC1 decline cross RSAFS increase) shows no statistically significant predictive power for 60-day forward returns (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: In a high-inflation environment, industrial production growth acts as a proxy for supply-side resilience, supporting corporate margins and driving positive equity returns over a one-week horizon.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth fails to predict positive 5-day forward returns when CPIAUCSL exceeds 195, showing a negative or zero correlation over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls signals robust aggregate demand and corporate hiring, which directly supports equity valuations over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows no statistically significant correlation with 60-day forward SPX returns (Rank IC < 0.02) over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained breach of the 4.5% federal funds rate threshold signals an aggressive tightening cycle that increases discount rates and compresses equity multiples, driving negative forward returns over a one-week horizon.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns are not significantly negative when FEDFUNDS breaches 4.5%, showing a positive or zero median return over a 3-year out-of-sample period.

mechanism_narrative: The simultaneous decline in industrial production and rise in the unemployment rate captures a synchronized supply-demand mismatch that historically precedes equity drawdowns, predicting negative returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The cross-product of INDPRO declines and UNRATE increases fails to predict negative 60-day forward returns (t-stat > -1.65) in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: In a high-interest-rate regime, rising consumer credit balances indicate resilient household spending that offsets monetary tightening, supporting positive equity returns over a monthly horizon.
failure_condition: RSAFS growth fails to predict positive 20-day forward returns when FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.5%, showing a negative or zero correlation over a 3-year rolling window.
